note: Honest coverage frontier. status: present = a runnable step drives real code and passes (must name a test); pending = specified behavior whose implementation (core/policy.Engine, Phase 2) does not exist yet; retrospective_gap = shipped without a driving test (must be zero for governed behavior, INV-22). The FOUNDATION leaves T-015-1 (embedded fixed OPA/Rego evaluator + deny-overrides) and T-015-2 (rule data model + verdict trinary + param inheritance) have landed, so their four scenarios are present and bound to spec/015-policy-engine/acceptance/acceptance_test.go; T-015-4 (four global modes: exactly-one-active, gated+audited transitions, fail-closed Shadow default) has landed and bound its three mode-state-machine scenarios (exactly-one-active, gated+audited transition, absent→Shadow) — its fourth scenario 'the mode is the sole actuation chokepoint that absorbs the retired gate' (REQ-1520) has now LANDED with the safety-core refactor T-015-13 (see below) and is present; T-015-6 (band composition — respect emits the more-restrictive of {verdict, risk band}, force overrides and double-warns, and neither breaches the INV-09 never-auto floor) has landed and bound its two band scenarios to spec/015-policy-engine/acceptance/acceptance_test.go; T-015-8 (per-op-class graduation ladder — promote to auto only after N verified-clean runs, demote on the first deviation, verify-on-auto bound so an unverified auto never counts) has landed and bound its two REQ-1514 promote/demote scenarios to spec/015-policy-engine/acceptance/acceptance_test.go — its third scenario 'An auto verdict still runs predict execute verify breaker' (REQ-1515) stays pending because it asserts the spec/013 predict→execute→verify→breaker ACTUATION chain, which this leaf deliberately does not wire (graduation only consumes the verifier's outcome via OutcomeFromVerdict; the actuation-chain wiring is a later integration leaf); T-015-7 (execution deny-floor + conservative/bare templates + removable-with-warning) has landed and bound its three scenarios to spec/015-policy-engine/acceptance/acceptance_test.go — the floor is an EXECUTION floor (the proposal is produced identically in every mode and preserved; only the auto-execute verdict is floored to deny, REQ-1511), the conservative template carries the predecessor safe-exec argv deny-patterns + a 30/min governor (REQ-1512), and a floor removal / bare allow-all is permitted-but-loud behind a distinct red double-confirmation that is audited while the constitutional mechanical never-auto floor (core/safety, INV-09) still clamps floor-class ops beneath the removed policy layer (REQ-1513); T-015-3 (min_confidence clamp + rate_limit governor) has landed and bound its two scenarios to spec/015-policy-engine/acceptance/acceptance_test.go — a below-min_confidence auto is clamped auto→approve by the pure ClampConfidence (REQ-1507) and the (N+1)th auto in the trailing window is clamped auto→approve by the concurrency-safe, clock-seam RateGovernor (REQ-1508); both clamps ONLY tighten (verdictRank(out) >= verdictRank(in), property-tested) and neither lifts the constitutional never-auto floor (core/safety, INV-09) beneath the engine; T-015-9 (local principals+groups + approve_by enforcement on /v1/vote, REQ-1516) has landed and bound its two vote scenarios (member admitted, non-member rejected) to spec/015-policy-engine/acceptance/acceptance_test.go — MayApprove admits a vote ONLY WHEN the voter's principal id or group is in the pending decision's approve_by set, resolving through the shared local-and-credential-human-plane PrincipalResolver (no forked identity store); empty approve_by and any resolver error fail closed; the third REQ-1516 scenario 'A federated principal resolves through the configured provider' stays pending until the federated LDAP/OIDC resolver leaf T-015-10 lands behind the same interface; T-015-1's Engine.Decide now COMPOSES the full most-restrictive-first decision procedure (design.md 'Mode/verdict decision procedure') — Rego deny-overrides base (a deny short-circuits) → EffectiveParams inheritance → confidence+rate clamps → band composition (with the inviolable INV-09 never-auto floor) → graduation → execution deny-floor → approve_by — and returns the REQUIRED-FIELD PolicyDecision (Verdict/MatchedRuleID/ComposedBand/ApproveBy/Mode + a non-secret audit projection), built only via NewPolicyDecision so a missing field is a compile error (the type-level half of REQ-1518's persistence contract, INV-19); the REQ-1503 (rules-as-data) and REQ-1507 (below-min-confidence clamp) scenarios now bind THROUGH the composed Engine.Decide (the confidence clamp resolves min_confidence via EffectiveParams and fires inside Decide, not an external Refine), and the full ordered chain is proven end-to-end by core/policy/engine_compose_test.go; the 'policy_decision table is append-only' scenario stays pending because the pgx migration is persistence (T-015-12); T-015-11 (warn-don't-block guard + audit-every-decision + admin engine toggle) has now landed and bound its three scenarios to spec/015-policy-engine/acceptance/acceptance_test.go — audit-every-decision is realized by AuditedEngine, which wraps (never alters) Engine.Decide and emits EXACTLY ONE non-secret decision record per evaluation (auto/approve/deny AND the engine-off deferral) to an AuditSink; a sink failure is best-effort but traced, never silently swallowed, and a missing sink is loud (REQ-1518, and the 'appended to the tamper-evident ledger' scenario binds through LedgerAuditSink onto the real hash-chained core/audit ledger); the warn-don't-block guard surfaces non-blocking PolicyWarnings for an allow-all rule / a removed floor / Full-auto and NEVER refuses the operator's chosen posture — a single allow-all is reachable behind the distinct red double-confirmation (REQ-1517); and the admin EngineToggle (authority-gated via the shared AuthorityChecker seam, every override audited to the governance ledger) records a warning when forcing the engine on in Shadow/HITL and a double-confirmation warning when disabling it in Semi-auto/Full-auto (REQ-1519), while engine-OFF defers to the mechanical floors (verdict approve, never auto; the constitutional never-auto floor still clamps beneath — engine-off is not a bypass); T-015-5 (identical-pipeline invariant guard — the mode governs ONLY the actuation branch, REQ-1501) has now landed and bound its scenario ('The investigation pipeline is identical across all four modes') to spec/015-policy-engine/acceptance/acceptance_test.go — core/policy.AssertModeInvariant re-runs the composed Engine.Decide across all four modes (varying only in.Mode) and requires the resolved verdict / composed band / approve_by to be IDENTICAL, permitting ONLY PolicyDecision.Mode (the recorded active-mode audit field) to differ; the invariant holds because Decide never branches on in.Mode (it composes the never-auto floor + Rego deny-overrides + confidence/rate clamps + band composition + graduation + execution deny-floor, all functions of the ACTION and the operator rule DATA, and merely CARRIES in.Mode into the decision for the audit — the mode's actuation semantics live at the chokepoint, T-015-13, strictly after Decide); the scenario's Then also drives a deliberately mode-dependent stub decider through the SAME guard and asserts it is DETECTED with the typed ErrPipelineModeDependent (a negative control proving the guard is not a vacuous no-op), and policy.SelfTest is the boot hook the preflight calls to REFUSE to start a worker whose pipeline is mode-dependent (fail closed, mirroring core/actuate.Interceptor.SelfTest); the remaining scenarios stay pending until their owning leaf (T-015-10, T-015-12, T-015-13) lands. T-015-13 (SAFETY-CORE REFACTOR: absorb MutationGate + TG_MUTATION_ENABLED into the mode chokepoint + wire policy.Engine.Decide into the actuation interceptor) has now landed and bound its two scenarios (REQ-1520 'the mode is the sole actuation chokepoint that absorbs the retired gate' and REQ-1521 'absorbing the mutation gate is a deliberate audited safety-core refactor') to spec/015-policy-engine/acceptance/acceptance_test.go — the retired core/safety.MutationGate object, the TG_MUTATION_ENABLED env knob, and the standalone console toggle are RETIRED and absorbed into core/safety.Chokepoint, whose sole authority safety.MayActuate(mode, preflightGreen) = mode in {Semi-auto,Full-auto} && preflightGreen answers 'may this actuate?' from the ONE source of truth (the mode); the zero/unknown mode is Shadow (fail-closed read-only), ProvePreflight marks the boot preflight green WITHOUT enabling (enabling is an authenticated, authority-checked, ledger-audited ModeController transition gated on that green preflight), and a deviation-breaker trip / POST /halt call Chokepoint.ForceShadow -> ModeController.ForceShadow (the absorbed gate.Disable); the interceptor's Do chain now consults policy.Engine.Decide (via the AuditedEngine) BEFORE the mode chokepoint as an INDEPENDENT control layer beneath which the never-auto floor (INV-09) and the mode chokepoint each fail closed (the negative control: no code path actuates at Shadow, proven in core/actuate/policy_wire_test.go and the REQ-1520 scenario's grep-style assertGateFullyRetired negative control that walks the runtime Go source and fails if any live MutationGate/TG_MUTATION_ENABLED definition remains); the absorption is re-expressed in mode-chokepoint terms across docs/adr/0013-mode-is-the-actuation-chokepoint.md, docs/CONSTITUTION.md, spec/013-actuation-interceptor/requirements.md, and .lockstep.lock, and mutation STAYS OFF (mode defaults Shadow). REQ-1522 (policy decision-surface hardening) has landed on the T-015-1 evaluator: every PolicyDecision now carries the loaded rule-bundle version (a deterministic content fingerprint of the rules-as-data, DecisionAudit.BundleVersion, surfaced via PolicyDecision.BundleVersion() and persisted into the governance-ledger row's serialized detail by LedgerAuditSink with NO new column or migration) and the FULL bounded/typed matched-rule list (DecisionAudit.MatchedRules — each matched rule id + its verdict, deny-overrides provenance, outcome unchanged); and a fixed-Rego evaluator ERROR now fails closed to `deny` (refuse), never to a permissive verdict and never to `auto` — the auditable bundle-version + matched-rule-list legs bind present through core/policy/acceptance's 'Every decision records the bundle version and the full matched-rule list' scenario, and the fail-to-deny-on-evaluator-error leg is locked white-box in core/policy/hardening_test.go (TestDecide_EvaluatorError_FailsClosedToDeny), which injects a failing evaluator through the unexported baseEvaluator seam because there is deliberately no public path from input to an evaluator error.

scenarios.name: Operator rules enter the fixed Rego module as data only
A matching deny wins regardless of rule order
A rule resolves an action to one of auto approve or deny
An unset rule param inherits from the global-default rule
A below-min-confidence action is clamped to approve
An over-rate-limit action is clamped to approve
Exactly one of the four modes is active
A mode transition is gated and audited to the ledger
The wired RBAC admits a flip-authorized operator to escalate with a green preflight
The wired RBAC denies and audits an unauthorized operator's flip
A flip is refused when the boot preflight is not green
An absent persisted mode fails closed to Shadow
The mode is the sole actuation chokepoint that absorbs the retired gate
Absorbing the mutation gate is a deliberate audited safety-core refactor
The investigation pipeline is identical across all four modes
Respect band_mode emits the more-restrictive of verdict and risk band
Force band_mode overrides the risk band and double-warns
A floor-class action is still proposed with rationale in every mode
The conservative template loads the predecessor deny-patterns and governor
Removing the conservative template warns but is not blocked
An op-class promotes to auto after N clean verified runs
An op-class demotes to approve on the first deviation
An auto verdict still runs predict execute verify breaker
A vote from an approve_by member is admitted
A vote from a non-member is rejected
A federated principal resolves through the configured provider
A single allow-all policy is permitted behind a red double-confirmation
Every policy decision is appended to the governance ledger
Forcing the engine on in Shadow warns and disabling it in Semi-auto double-warns
The console edits rules as data and renders the packet-tracer explanation
The policy_decision table is append-only with no runtime UPDATE or DELETE
Every decision records the bundle version and the full matched-rule list
